original_image_tags: Aged, off-white, rectangular envelope, likely the back/flap side. The envelope is sealed with blue crayon or pencil lines forming a large 'X' across the surface. There is a circular black stamped seal on the left side, a circular red stamped seal in the center, and a circular black stamped seal on the right side. A small, dark hole is visible on the upper right edge.

elements.description: Scanned image of the back of a white envelope. Contains three circular ink stamps: a purple center stamp reading 'WASHINGTON, D.C. / NOV 18 1954 / REG. DIV.'; a left black stamp reading 'CHORGCAIGHE / N-I-NGARBH CC / 28 15 4'; a right black stamp reading 'LE' ANNRACUI CHORGCAIGHE'. A blue crayon cross is drawn over the envelope, and a hole punch is visible on the right edge.

judge.reason: The descriptions disagree on the color of the left and center stamps (black vs. purple for center, black vs. black for left) and contain contradictory verbatim text for the right stamp ('right black stamped seal' vs. 'right black stamp reading "LE' ANNRACUI CHORGCAIGHE"').
